description: go - Go is a tool for managing Go source code.

root.name: bug
tip: start a bug report

root.name: build
tip: compile packages and dependencies

option.name: -a
tip: force rebuilding of packages that are already up-to-date.

option.name: -asan
tip: enable interoperation with address sanitizer.
Supported only on linux/arm64, linux/amd64, linux/loong64.
Supported on linux/amd64 or linux/arm64 and only with GCC 7 and higher or Clang/LLVM 9 and higher.
And supported on linux/loong64 only with Clang/LLVM 16 and higher.

option.name: -asmflags
tip: arguments to pass on each go tool asm invocation.

option.name: -buildmode
tip: build mode to use. See 'go help buildmode' for more.

option.name: -buildvcs
tip: Whether to stamp binaries with version control information ("true", "false", or "auto").
By default ("auto"), version control information is stamped if the main package and repo structure allow.
Use -buildvcs=false to always omit, or -buildvcs=true to error out if information cannot be included.

option.name: -C
tip: Change to dir before running the command.
Any files named on the command line are interpreted after changing directories.
If used, this flag must be the first one in the command line.

option.name: -compiler
tip: name of compiler to use, as in runtime.Compiler (gccgo or gc).

option.name: -cover
tip: enable code coverage instrumentation.

option.name: -covermode
tip: set the mode for coverage analysis.
The default is "set" unless -race is enabled, in which case it is "atomic".
The values: set (does this statement run?), count (how many times?), atomic (count, but correct in multithreaded tests).
Sets -cover.

option.name: -coverpkg
tip: For a build that targets package 'main', apply coverage analysis to each package whose import path matches the patterns.
The default is to apply coverage analysis to packages in the main Go module.
Sets -cover.

option.name: -gccgoflags
tip: arguments to pass on each gccgo compiler/linker invocation.

option.name: -gcflags
tip: arguments to pass on each go tool compile invocation.

option.name: -installsuffix
tip: a suffix to use in the name of the package installation directory to keep output separate.
If using -race, -msan, or -asan, the suffix is automatically set or appended.

option.name: -json
tip: Emit build output in JSON suitable for automated processing.

option.name: -ldflags
tip: arguments to pass on each go tool link invocation.

option.name: -linkshared
tip: build code that will be linked against shared libraries previously created with -buildmode=shared.

option.name: -mod
tip: module download mode to use: readonly, vendor, or mod.
Default is 'vendor' if a vendor directory exists and go version >= 1.14; otherwise 'readonly'.

option.name: -modcacherw
tip: leave newly-created directories in the module cache read-write instead of making them read-only.

option.name: -modfile
tip: read (and possibly write) an alternate go.mod file instead of the one in the module root directory.
An alternate go.sum file is also used, derived from the -modfile path.

option.name: -msan
tip: enable interoperation with memory sanitizer.
Supported only on linux/amd64, linux/arm64, linux/loong64, freebsd/amd64 and only with Clang/LLVM as the host C compiler.
PIE build mode will be used on all platforms except linux/amd64.

option.name: -n
tip: print the commands but do not run them.

option.name: -o
tip: set the output path of executable file.

option.name: -overlay
tip: read a JSON config file that provides an overlay for build operations (mapping disk paths to backing files).
Has limitations regarding cgo files, 'go run/test', and GOMODCACHE.

option.name: -p
tip: the number of programs, such as build commands or test binaries, that can be run in parallel.
The default is GOMAXPROCS, normally the number of CPUs available.

option.name: -pgo
tip: specify the file path of a profile for profile-guided optimization (PGO).
"auto" selects "default.pgo" in the package directory if it exists. "off" turns off PGO.

option.name: -pkgdir
tip: install and load all packages from dir instead of the usual locations.

option.name: -race
tip: enable data race detection.
Supported only on darwin/amd64, darwin/arm64, freebsd/amd64, linux/amd64, linux/arm64 (only for 48-bit VMA), linux/ppc64le, linux/riscv64 and windows/amd64.

option.name: -tags
tip: a comma-separated list of additional build tags to consider satisfied during the build.

option.name: -toolexec
tip: a program to use to invoke toolchain programs like vet and asm.
The TOOLEXEC_IMPORTPATH environment variable will be set for the package being built.

option.name: -trimpath
tip: remove all file system paths from the resulting executable.
Recorded file names will begin with module path@version or a plain import path.

option.name: -v
tip: print the names of packages as they are compiled.

option.name: -work
tip: print the name of the temporary work directory and do not delete it when exiting.

option.name: -x
tip: print the commands.

root.name: clean
tip: remove object files and cached files

option.name: -cache
tip: causes clean to remove the entire go build cache

option.name: -fuzzcache
tip: causes clean to remove files stored in the Go build cache for fuzz testing
The fuzzing engine caches files that expand code coverage, so removing them may make fuzzing less effective until new inputs are found that provide the same coverage
These files are distinct from those stored in testdata directory; clean does not remove those files

option.name: -i
tip: causes clean to remove the corresponding installed archive or binary (what 'go install' would create)

option.name: -modcache
tip: causes clean to remove the entire module download cache, including unpacked source code of versioned dependencies

option.name: -n
tip: causes clean to print the remove commands it would execute, but not run them

option.name: -r
tip: causes clean to be applied recursively to all the dependencies of the packages named by the import paths

option.name: -testcache
tip: causes clean to expire all test results in the go build cache

option.name: -x
tip: causes clean to print remove commands as it executes them

root.name: doc
tip: show documentation for package or symbol

option.name: -all
tip: Show all the documentation for the package.

option.name: -c
tip: Respect case when matching symbols.

option.name: -cmd
tip: Treat a command (package main) like a regular package.
Otherwise package main's exported symbols are hidden when showing the package's top-level documentation.

option.name: -http
tip: Serve HTML docs over HTTP.

option.name: -short
tip: One-line representation for each symbol.

option.name: -src
tip: Show the full source code for the symbol.
This will display the full Go source of its declaration and definition, such as a function definition (including the body), type declaration or enclosing const block.
The output may therefore include unexported details.

option.name: -u
tip: Show documentation for unexported as well as exported symbols, methods, and fields.

root.name: env
tip: print Go environment information

option.name: -changed
tip: prints only those settings whose effective value differs from the default value that would be obtained in an empty environment with no prior uses of the -w flag

option.name: -json
tip: prints the environment in JSON format instead of as a shell script

option.name: -u
tip: requires one or more arguments and unsets the default setting for the named environment variables, if one has been set with 'go env -w'

option.name: -w
tip: requires one or more arguments of the form NAME=VALUE and changes the default settings of the named environment variables to the given values

root.name: fix
tip: apply fixes suggested by static checkers

option.name: -diff
tip: instead of applying each fix, print the patch as a unified diff

option.name: -fixtool
tip: selects a different analysis tool with alternative or additional fixers
see the documentation for go vet's -vettool flag for details

root.name: fmt
tip: gofmt (reformat) package sources

option.name: -mod
tip: sets which module download mode to use: readonly or vendor
See 'go help modules' for more

option.name: -n
tip: prints commands that would be executed

option.name: -x
tip: prints commands as they are executed

root.name: generate
tip: generate Go files by processing source

root.name: get
tip: add dependencies to current module and install them

option.name: -t
tip: instructs get to consider modules needed to build tests of packages specified on the command line
when used with -u, get will update test dependencies as well

option.name: -tool
tip: instructs go to add a matching tool line to go.mod for each listed package
if used with @none, the line will be removed

option.name: -u
tip: instructs get to update modules providing dependencies of packages named on the command line to use newer minor or patch releases when available
when used with -t, get will update test dependencies as well

option.name: -u=patch
tip: instructs get to update dependencies, but changes the default to select patch releases

option.name: -x
tip: prints commands as they are executed
useful for debugging version control commands when a module is downloaded directly from a repository

root.name: help
tip: get more information about a command

next.name: buildconstraint
tip: build constraints

next.name: buildjson
tip: build -json encoding

next.name: buildmode
tip: build modes

next.name: c
tip: calling between Go and C

next.name: cache
tip: build and test caching

next.name: environment
tip: environment variables

next.name: filetype
tip: file types

next.name: go.mod
tip: the go.mod file

next.name: goauth
tip: GOAUTH environment variable

next.name: gopath
tip: GOPATH environment variable

next.name: goproxy
tip: module proxy protocol

next.name: importpath
tip: import path syntax

next.name: module-auth
tip: module authentication using go.sum

next.name: modules
tip: modules, module versions, and more

next.name: packages
tip: package lists and patterns

next.name: private
tip: configuration for downloading non-public code

next.name: testflag
tip: testing flags

next.name: testfunc
tip: testing functions

next.name: vcs
tip: controlling version control with GOVCS

root.name: install
tip: compile and install packages and dependencies

root.name: list
tip: list packages or modules

option.name: -compiled
tip: causes list to set CompiledGoFiles to the Go source files presented to the compiler
adds Go code generated by processing CgoFiles and SwigFiles
Imports list will contain the union of all imports from both GoFiles and CompiledGoFiles

option.name: -deps
tip: causes list to iterate over not just the named packages but also all their dependencies
visits them in a depth-first post-order traversal
packages not explicitly listed on the command line will have the DepOnly field set to true

option.name: -e
tip: changes the handling of erroneous packages (those that cannot be found or are malformed)
never prints errors to standard error and instead processes the erroneous packages with the usual printing
erroneous packages will have a non-empty ImportPath and a non-nil Error field

option.name: -export
tip: causes list to set the Export field to the name of a file containing up-to-date export information for the given package
sets the BuildID field to the build ID of the compiled package

option.name: -find
tip: causes list to identify the named packages but not resolve their dependencies
Imports and Deps lists will be empty
cannot be used with -deps, -test, or -export

option.name: -json
tip: causes the package data to be printed in JSON format instead of using the template format
can optionally be provided with a set of comma-separated required field names to be output

option.name: -m
tip: causes list to list modules instead of packages
changes the context of other flags (like -f and -json) to use a Module struct instead of a Package struct

option.name: -retracted
tip: causes list to report information about retracted module versions
when used with -f or -json, the Retracted field explains the reason for retraction
can be used with or without -m

option.name: -reuse
tip: accepts the name of a file containing JSON output from a previous 'go list -m -json' invocation
avoids redownloading information if the module is unchanged
modules not redownloaded will have the Reuse field set to true
should be used with -m

option.name: -test
tip: reports not only the named packages but also their test binaries
reported import path for a test binary has a '.test' suffix
sets the ForTest field to the name of the package being tested

option.name: -u
tip: adds information about available upgrades for modules
sets the Module's Update field if a newer version is available
sets the Retracted field if the current version is retracted

option.name: -versions
tip: sets the Module's Versions field to a list of all known versions of that module
changes the default output format to display the module path followed by a space-separated version list

root.name: mod
tip: module maintenance

next.name: download
tip: download modules to local cache

next.name: edit
tip: edit go.mod from tools or scripts

next.name: graph
tip: print module requirement graph

next.name: init
tip: initialize new module in current directory

next.name: tidy
tip: add missing and remove unused modules

next.name: vendor
tip: make vendored copy of dependencies

next.name: verify
tip: verify dependencies have expected content

next.name: why
tip: explain why packages or modules are needed

root.name: run
tip: compile and run Go program

option.name: -exec
tip: invokes the binary using the xprog program
the command format used is 'xprog a.out arguments...'

root.name: telemetry
tip: manage telemetry data and settings

root.name: test
tip: test packages

option.name: -args
tip: Pass the remainder of the command line (everything after -args) to the test binary, uninterpreted and unchanged
Because this flag consumes the remainder of the command line, the package list (if present) must appear before this flag

option.name: -c
tip: Compile the test binary to pkg.test in the current directory but do not run it
The file name or target directory can be changed with the -o flag

option.name: -exec
tip: Run the test binary using the specified program (xprog)
The behavior is the same as in 'go run'

option.name: -json
tip: Convert test output to JSON suitable for automated processing
Emits build output in JSON as well

option.name: -o
tip: Save a copy of the test binary to the named file
The test still runs unless -c is specified
If the target is a directory, the binary is written to pkg.test in that directory

root.name: tool
tip: run specified go tool

option.name: -modfile
tip: causes tool to use an alternate file instead of the go.mod in the module root directory

option.name: -n
tip: causes tool to print the command that would be executed but not execute it

root.name: version
tip: print Go version

option.name: -c
tip: display offending line with this many lines of context
default value is -1

option.name: -diff
tip: instead of applying each fix, print the patch as a unified diff

option.name: -fix
tip: instead of printing each diagnostic, apply its first fix (if any)

option.name: -json
tip: emit JSON output

root.name: vet
tip: report likely mistakes in packages

root.name: work
tip: workspace maintenance

next.name: edit
tip: edit go.work from tools or scripts

next.name: init
tip: initialize workspace file

next.name: sync
tip: sync workspace build list to modules

next.name: use
tip: add modules to workspace file

next.name: vendor
tip: make vendored copy of dependencies
